directions.label: in-plane 100
directions.status: supported as 2D in-plane high-symmetry shorthand; named mode deferred to Task M

directions.label: in-plane 110
directions.status: supported as 2D in-plane high-symmetry shorthand; named mode deferred to Task M

Tier A.meaning: Current reliable raw-angle, 2D in-plane high-symmetry forward paths.

directions.label: generic in-plane
directions.status: computable but not yet promoted to broadly reliable truth-mode directions
directions.supported_as: raw interface_angle in the 2D kx-ky plane

Tier B.meaning: Computable continuous in-plane raw angles that require caution until dense Task-N validation.

directions.label: c-axis
directions.status: unsupported; the current interface geometry has no kz, out-of-plane velocity, or c-axis injection path

Tier C.meaning: Not physically implemented in the current forward model.

final_verdict: The current forward truth chain has a validated 2D in-plane raw-angle interface path. In-plane 100 and 110 labels are acceptable shorthand for high-symmetry raw angles, generic in-plane raw angles are computable but should be treated cautiously until Task N, and c-axis transport is not physically implemented.

semantic_contract.dimensionality: strictly 2D in-plane in the current implementation
semantic_contract.interface_angle_definition: 2D in-plane polar angle, in radians, of the interface normal in the model kx-ky plane.
semantic_contract.reflection_rule: k_out = k_in - 2 * (k_in dot normal) * normal, wrapped to the square Brillouin zone

support_status.100: Supported only as 2D in-plane shorthand for raw angles 0 and pi/2.
support_status.110: Supported only as 2D in-plane shorthand for raw angle pi/4.
support_status.c-axis: Not supported by the current forward model.
